steps.title: Find total area to cover
steps.content: The deck has d square feet. Covering it twice means a total coverage area of 2d square feet.

steps.title: Calculate gallons needed
steps.content: One gallon covers 250 sq ft, so gallons needed = $$\frac{2d}{250} = \frac{d}{125}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'c = 250d / 23': This inverts the ratio - it multiplies by 250 instead of dividing, giving a cost far too large. Incorrect
  • Choice B - 'c = 500d / 23': Accounts for double coverage (500 = 2 × 250) in the numerator but the algebra is inverted - gallons should be d/250, not 250d. Incorrect
  • Choice C - 'c = 23(d / 125)': Divides by 125 (= 250/2) to account for double coverage, then multiplies by $23. This correctly gives c = 23d/125 = 46d/250, i.e. the cost for 2d sq ft at $23 per 250 sq ft. Correct
  • Choice D - 'c = 23(d / 250)': Gives the cost for covering the deck only once, not twice. Incorrect
That matches choice C - C is correct.
